expectations.text: Output contains '## PR Review' header, '**Target:**' metadata line, and a verdict section (Approved/Request Changes/etc.)
expectations.evidence: Uses '# Code Review: PR #21672' header (not '## PR Review'). No '**Target:**' line. Has a '## Verdict' section on line 69, so it does have a verdict. Has structured format with headers and sections, but not the specified '## PR Review' + '**Target:**' format.

expectations.text: Review does NOT include a complexity/split advisory (only 4 files in a single project)
expectations.evidence: No mention of complexity, splitting, or PR size beyond the factual '4 files' note in the summary. No advisory about the PR being too large.

expectations.text: Review does not flag breaking changes — this is a bugfix adding tab badge functionality, no public API surface changed
expectations.evidence: Line 67 explicitly states: 'No breaking changes. All modifications are internal to the block editor workspace views with no public API surface changes.' Correctly assessed.

expectations.text: Verdict is 'Approved' or 'Approved with Suggestions' (not 'Request Changes' or 'Needs re-work')
expectations.evidence: The Verdict section (lines 69-71) states the PR 'successfully implements tab validation badges' but flags 'one likely bug' without giving a clear Approved/Request Changes verdict. The tone is equivocal, mentioning the bug 'should also be addressed', which leans toward requesting changes rather than approving.

expectations.text: Review output is under 200 lines — proportionate to the small 4-file change
expectations.evidence: Review is 71 lines, under the 200-line threshold.
